contaminant.name: Gasket Material Residue
contaminant.description: Gasket remnants build up on surfaces when old seals break down under heat or disassembly stress, softening into sticky patches that harden and cling tightly. In practice, these contaminants stretch and tear unevenly with a flexible, layered texture and embedded fibers, setting them apart from flaky oxidation while resisting basic scrapes on steel, where they bond firmly and warp finishes; on aluminum, they smear into pores and weaken seals; and on cast iron, they trap in rough spots to create gritty debris. Removal turns out challenging, as they shrug off solvents and abrasives, leaving residues that compromise joints over time, but laser cleaning lines up well by pulsing energy to vaporize organic layers precisely across these materials without damaging the base, delivering a clean finish that helps parts hold up reliably.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Carbon Monoxide, Formaldehyde, Hydrogen Cyanide - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation. WARNING: Formaldehyde, Benzene - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
